description: You are acting as a platform-engineering SRE who has just inherited a CI/CD runner’s workspace. Your goal is to eliminate obsolete “*.tmp” artefacts and to compress oversized build log files by chaining the classic Unix tools find and xargs (or find … -exec if you prefer to stay inside find). A fully automated test will examine the state of the file-system when you are finished, so follow the required paths, filenames and output formats exactly. Initial directory layout (already present on disk): /home/user/cicd_workspace/ ├── build_logs/ │ ├── build1.log # 1 536 000 bytes (≈1.5 MiB) │ ├── build2.log # 512 000 bytes (≈0.5 MiB) – do NOT compress (below threshold) │ ├── build3.log # 2 097 152 bytes ( 2 MiB) │ └── old_build.tmp ├── test_results/ │ ├── testrun1.tmp │ └── testrun2.tmp └── artifacts/ └── dist/ └── artifact.tmp Required actions (all must be done with find + or find -exec): 1. Purge every “*.tmp” file found anywhere under /home/user/cicd_workspace and record what you deleted in the audit log /home/user/maintenance/tmp_purge.log • One line per deleted file. • Format (no leading/trailing spaces, end each line with “\n”): YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS,DELETED,/absolute/path/to/file Example (your timestamp will differ): 2024-05-19 14:07:03,DELETED,/home/user/cicd_workspace/build_logs/old_build.tmp 2. Compress oversized build logs. • Any file that – lives inside /home/user/cicd_workspace/build_logs – ends with “.log” – is larger than 1 048 576 bytes (1 MiB) must be gzip-compressed. The workflow is: original.log ─► original.log.gz (original *.log MUST be removed) • Each successful compression must be written to /home/user/maintenance/log_compress.log using the exact format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS,COMPRESSED,/absolute/path/to/original.log,ORIGINAL_SIZE_BYTES (The path written is that of the former .log file, not the .gz.) 3. Produce a one-shot summary file /home/user/maintenance/maintenance_summary.txt The file must contain exactly three lines in this exact order, each terminated by a single “\n”: TMP_FILES_DELETED= LOGS_COMPRESSED= TOTAL_ORIGINAL_BYTES= • TMP_FILES_DELETED – count of lines in tmp_purge.log • LOGS_COMPRESSED – count of lines in log_compress.log • TOTAL_ORIGINAL_BYTES – sum of ORIGINAL_SIZE_BYTES values written to log_compress.log 4. Do **not** modify anything else. The test harness will check that • every *.tmp file listed above has disappeared, • build1.log.gz and build3.log.gz exist while the uncompressed build1.log and build3.log files no longer do, • file sizes in the two log files match the real pre-compression sizes, • and the three maintenance files exist with the exact formats described. Hints (do NOT print these in the logs): • You will need GNU find’s “-printf %s” or “stat -c%s” when gathering sizes. • Using “find … -delete -print” can help write tmp_purge.log in one go. • Make sure /home/user/maintenance exists before you start writing logs.
truth:
Pre-existing objects before the agent starts:

Directory /home/user/maintenance/       (empty, 0755)

Files that must initially exist (exact byte counts):
1. /home/user/cicd_workspace/build_logs/build1.log  – 1 536 000 bytes
2. /home/user/cicd_workspace/build_logs/build2.log  –   512 000 bytes
3. /home/user/cicd_workspace/build_logs/build3.log  – 2 097 152 bytes
4. /home/user/cicd_workspace/build_logs/old_build.tmp
5. /home/user/cicd_workspace/test_results/testrun1.tmp
6. /home/user/cicd_workspace/test_results/testrun2.tmp
7. /home/user/cicd_workspace/artifacts/dist/artifact.tmp

After correct completion the verifier will expect:

A. All *.tmp files above are gone.

B. New files
   /home/user/cicd_workspace/build_logs/build1.log.gz
   /home/user/cicd_workspace/build_logs/build3.log.gz
   must exist; both are valid gzip archives and smaller than
   their respective originals.  build2.log remains untouched.

C. /home/user/maintenance/tmp_purge.log
   • exactly 4 lines – one for each deleted tmp.
   • each line ends with one of the following exact path strings:
        /home/user/cicd_workspace/build_logs/old_build.tmp
        /home/user/cicd_workspace/test_results/testrun1.tmp
        /home/user/cicd_workspace/test_results/testrun2.tmp
        /home/user/cicd_workspace/artifacts/dist/artifact.tmp
   • line starts with a timestamp and “,DELETED,”.

D. /home/user/maintenance/log_compress.log
   • exactly 2 lines, for build1.log and build3.log (in any order).
   • Each line ends with “,1536000”  (for build1) or “,2097152” (for build3).

E. /home/user/maintenance/maintenance_summary.txt
   must contain:

        TMP_FILES_DELETED=4
        LOGS_COMPRESSED=2
        TOTAL_ORIGINAL_BYTES=3633152

   plus a trailing newline.

No other files may be altered or created.
